summary: Full backend + frontend test for ParkWise-AI. All 12 backend pytest cases and all 8 Playwright UI flows passed. Header shows '40 Risk Zones' / '8,222 Violations'. Map renders 40 Leaflet markers with density/impact toggle, zone detail panel opens on marker click. Dashboard renders all KPIs. Recommendation Center renders 40 zone cards with rule-engine recommendations and a working Gemini 'Explain Risk' button that streams Risk Summary / Key Risk Factors / Recommended Actions / Recommendation Explanation / Expected Benefit. Gemini caching verified (second POST returns identical content).

issues: Recharts console warnings: 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0'. Charts still render correctly once container is measured, but this indicates a brief initial mount where ResponsiveContainer has no size. Cosmetic only.

'Frequent Violation' and 'Hidden Risk' cards show 0 with current seed data — visually OK but suggests the classification engine never tags any of the 40 seed zones with these two classes. Not a bug in this scope but worth E1 verifying classification thresholds in /app/backend/recommendations.py.

action_items: (Optional) Fix Recharts 'width(-1) height(-1)' warnings by giving ResponsiveContainer parents an explicit min-height/min-width.
(Optional) Review classification thresholds so 'Frequent Violation' / 'Hidden Risk' buckets are non-empty with the 40 seed zones, or document why they are intentionally empty.
critical_code_review_comments: main.py uses module-level mutable globals HOTSPOTS/RECOMMENDATIONS and an in-memory AI cache attached to the dict — fine for single-replica preview, but will not be shared across workers or survive restarts. Acceptable per problem statement (file-based persistence), just call this out.

gemini_engine._call_gemini_sync spawns a thread per request with a fresh event loop; works, but blocks a worker thread for up to 60s. Consider making /api/recommendation/{id}/explain truly async by awaiting LlmChat directly from FastAPI's event loop.
CORS allows '*' with allow_credentials=True (line 22-26 of main.py) — browsers will silently reject credentialed requests; not currently used by frontend but worth tightening.
/app/backend/main.py at ~440 lines is still manageable but is starting to mix data loading, all 8 endpoints and the pipeline trigger. Split routers when adding more endpoints.

No data-testid on Sidebar nav buttons or MapView toggle; they use id='nav-*' / id='toggle-*' instead. Tests work via id, but standardising on data-testid would match the rest of the app (App.js already uses data-testid).
Dropdown of seed_data.py and data_processor.py was not exercised (POST /api/process not invoked in this run because the dataset is already seeded and re-running may overwrite the in-memory AI cache).
test_credentials: None required — no auth in app.
seed_data_creation: Backend already seeds 40 Bengaluru zones into /app/backend/data/{hotspots,recommendations}.json on first start via seed_data.ensure_seed_data().
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_parkwise_api.py (12 tests, ~16s incl. one live Gemini call). Public URL is REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Gemini explanation cache is per-process and per-zone (in HOTSPOTS list in memory). Calling /api/recommendation/{id}/explain twice returns identical JSON — used for cache test. Avoid running POST /api/process during tests; it rewrites the JSON files. Sidebar buttons use id='nav-{map|dashboard|recommendations|about}', map toggles use id='toggle-density-map' / '#toggle-impact-map', and ZoneDetails panel root is id='zone-detail-panel'. Each recommendation card has class 'explain-risk-btn'.
rca of the issue: No bugs found in this iteration.
